weather_line: Fog heavy in the hollows · middle D persists · ducks probable

stories.headline: Map Fragment Authenticated: The Tiny Duck Chart Points to Something Real
stories.byline: By our Highland Correspondent, filed from a ridge above Slade
body: Cartographers at the Department for the Regulation and Control of Magical Creatures confirmed Tuesday morning what Steve had suspected since Sunday night: the hand-drawn map delivered by the seventh duck is, against all reasonable expectation, accurate. Every ridge line, hollow, and creek crossing corresponds precisely to a trail segment in the enchanted Appalachian highlands — specifically a route northeast of the Gorge that experienced trackers describe as 'the kind of path that finds you, not the other way round.'

The gold duck silhouette stamped on the map's lower corner has been cross-referenced against fourteen known enchantment registries and the full back-catalogue of 'Duck, Duck, Jeep' ritual notices. It matches nothing on file, which the Department considers 'extremely interesting' and Steve considers 'a good reason to air up the tyres.' Christine, who has been known to out-read a goblin cartography team before her second coffee, traced the route with one finger and said only: 'Thursday.'
The Jeep's engine reportedly turned over on the first try this morning, unprompted, at precisely the moment Christine spoke. Analysts are calling it a coincidence. The Jeep is calling it loyalty.

stories.headline: Vending Machine Produces Second Unsolicited Item; This One Has a Ribbon On It
body: The Level Six vending machine — now widely referred to among junior Ministry staff as 'the Oracle' — dispensed a second unrequested item at 8:47 Tuesday morning: a small sealed envelope tied with a gold ribbon, addressed in handwriting that two graphology experts called 'elegant' and one called 'frankly smug.'

The envelope has not been opened. It now sits beside the briefcase, which continues to hum in middle D. Observers note the hum has acquired a faint harmony — a perfect fifth above the original note — since the envelope arrived. A misfiled interdepartmental memo, recovered this morning from behind a radiator on Level Three, reads in full: 'Re: the thing in the case — it was always going to end up with her. Stop fussing.' The memo is undated. The signature is illegible. The Ministry has stamped it 'not our department' and returned it to the radiator.

stories.headline: Sub-Committee Produces Seventeen-Page Addendum; Christine Summarises It in Four Words
body: The Goblin Sub-Committee on Fiduciary Oversight, convened in emergency session Monday and apparently still going, circulated a seventeen-page procedural addendum to Christine's office at half past nine Tuesday. It proposed fourteen new audit triggers, three revised counter-signature clauses, and one entirely new form (Form 7-Theta, 'Declaration of Intent to Declare').

Christine read the document, set it down, and dictated a four-word reply that this paper is not at liberty to print in full, but which sources confirm began with 'Absolutely' and ended with 'not.' By eleven o'clock the Sub-Committee had withdrawn eight of the fourteen triggers, tabled the counter-signature clauses pending 'further reflection,' and quietly shelved Form 7-Theta entirely. Head Sub-Comptroller Graknik was seen afterward staring at a wall, eating a biscuit very slowly, with the look of a goblin who has been outmanoeuvred and knows it and is trying to decide if he minds.

stories.headline: Dispatch Received From the Far Correspondent: Terse, Warm, Contains a Question About the Dogs
stories.byline: Andrew reporting, from somewhere considerably further away
body: A brief dispatch arrived Tuesday from the Prophet's foreign correspondent, postmarked from a longitude he declines to specify. It consisted of three lines: an inquiry as to whether Ellie had 'sorted out the mirror situation yet,' a note that the weather abroad is 'fine, unremarkable, not the Gorge,' and a closing remark expressing confidence that whatever the Sub-Committee tried this week, Christine had 'already seen it coming.' He is, as usual, correct on all counts. The dispatch smelled faintly of something tropical and arrived completely dry despite a reported storm at sea.

familiars_corner: Lucy: Investigators report that the biscuit tin was found open this morning, three biscuits missing, and Lucy asleep eighteen inches away with an expression of complete moral innocence; the Improper Use of Canine Alibi Office has opened a file but concedes, again, that there is no evidence.

Ellie: Ellie spent forty-five minutes this morning making friends with the new duck (the one with the map), introducing herself twice, and appeared genuinely surprised both times; she has since relocated to the garden to investigate a leaf, which she regards as suspicious.

Jersey: Jersey has identified the precise square inch of the sofa that receives the longest unbroken patch of Tuesday afternoon sun, and has secured it; he does not consider this an achievement so much as his natural due.
founding_editor_note: That Sub-Committee addendum was seventeen pages because they used the wrong margins — I could've told them one page, proper margins, does the job; Christine already knew that, of course. — D.

in_brief.sport: Gringotts Inter-Vault Weightlifting Invitational confirms Christine's entry form is 'technically the most correctly filed document we have ever received,' which is not a category, but perhaps should be.
in_brief.forecast: Fog holds through Wednesday; ridgelines clearer by Thursday, as if something has been expecting that.

storyline_updates.title: The Duck Bandit
storyline_updates.summary: The seventh duck's map has been authenticated by Ministry cartographers — it accurately charts a real trail northeast of the Gorge. Steve is preparing the Jeep. Christine has said 'Thursday.' The Jeep started on its own when she said it. The Bandit appears to have been leading them somewhere specific all along; the trail destination and the Bandit's identity are yet to be revealed.

storyline_updates.title: The Briefcase Mystery
storyline_updates.summary: A sealed gold-ribboned envelope appeared from the vending machine, addressed in smug elegant handwriting, and now sits beside the briefcase. The hum has gained a second note — a perfect fifth harmony. A recovered memo from Level Three states 'it was always going to end up with her' and refers to the case's contents as 'the thing.' The Ministry has re-filed the memo behind the radiator. Contents still unknown; resolution drawing close.
